evals.name: sp500-tech-sector
evals.prompt: I want to find some US tech stocks that are not too expensive and have solid profitability. PE under 25, ROE at least 20%. What are the best picks right now?
evals.expected_output: Uses server-side screening via yf.screen(), applies Technology sector + PE/ROE filters with balanced style, returns ranked list with 5-factor scores and industry breakdown

assertions.name: server_side_screening
assertions.description: Uses server-side API, not scanning 500+ tickers individually

assertions.name: sector_filter_applied
assertions.description: Technology sector filter applied correctly

assertions.name: multiple_results
assertions.description: Returns multiple stocks (not just 1-2)

assertions.name: five_factor_scoring
assertions.description: Output shows 5-factor scoring with valuation, profitability, growth, momentum, safety

assertions.name: score_differentiation
assertions.description: Clear score ranking with meaningful spread

evals.name: hstech-quality
evals.prompt: Help me look at the Hang Seng Tech index — which stocks in it have good fundamentals? I care about profitability and valuation being reasonable.
evals.expected_output: Uses --index hstech with quality or balanced style, screens 27 HK tickers, returns quality-ranked list with .HK format

assertions.name: hstech_index_used
assertions.description: Used --index hstech, loaded 27 tickers

assertions.name: hk_tickers_present
assertions.description: All results use .HK ticker format

assertions.name: style_appropriate
assertions.description: Uses quality or balanced style given user emphasis on profitability + valuation

assertions.name: score_differentiation
assertions.description: Clear score ranking

assertions.name: csv_output_exists
assertions.description: CSV output file produced

evals.name: custom-8stocks
evals.prompt: I'm watching these stocks: AAPL MSFT NVDA TSLA META AMZN GOOGL JPM. Which ones are actually good value right now? Filter out anything with PE over 40 or ROE below 10%.
evals.expected_output: Screens all 8 tickers with --pe 40 --roe 10 and value style (user says 'good value'), TSLA filtered out, remaining stocks ranked by 5-factor score

assertions.name: all_tickers_screened
assertions.description: All 8 tickers were screened

assertions.name: value_style_used
assertions.description: Uses value style preset since user explicitly asks about value

assertions.name: tsla_filtered_out
assertions.description: TSLA excluded by filters

assertions.name: five_factor_output
assertions.description: Output includes PE, EV/EBITDA, ROE, growth, momentum, D/E columns

assertions.name: score_differentiation
assertions.description: Clear score ranking with meaningful spread

evals.name: growth-screening
evals.prompt: Find me high-growth US tech stocks. I don't mind paying up for growth — PE up to 50 is fine, ROE at least 10%.
evals.expected_output: Uses --sector Technology --pe 50 --roe 10 --style growth, returns stocks ranked with growth-weighted scoring emphasizing revenue/earnings growth and momentum

assertions.name: growth_style_used
assertions.description: Uses growth style preset for growth-focused request

assertions.name: relaxed_pe_filter
assertions.description: PE threshold set to 50 as user specified

assertions.name: growth_weighted_scoring
assertions.description: Scoring weights show Growth(40%) + Momentum(25%) emphasis

assertions.name: multiple_results
assertions.description: Returns multiple stocks

assertions.name: score_differentiation
assertions.description: Clear score ranking
